title: oncosiphon glabratus
intro: Oncosiphon glabratus, also known as Cancer Bush, is a plant species native to the Western Cape province of South Africa, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and aromatic leaves. Traditionally, Oncosiphon glabratus has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Oncosiphon glabratus is often used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
